capabilities.name: Bill of Materials (BOM)
capabilities.description: Structured definition of the components, quantities, and assembly relationships that make up a manufactured product. Foundation for every other manufacturing function.

requirements.text: Support multi-level BOM structure with parent/child/sub-assembly hierarchy to represent complex product compositions
proxy_refs: Multi-level BOM with parent/child/sub-assembly hierarchy

requirements.text: Define component quantities with unit-of-measure and scrap/waste factors for accurate material planning
proxy_refs: Component quantities with unit-of-measure and scrap factor

proxy_refs: Alternate / substitute components with substitution rules

requirements.text: Support phantom sub-assemblies that explode through to lower levels without creating intermediate inventory
proxy_refs: Phantom sub-assemblies (explode-through components for lean production)

requirements.text: Capture reference designators to specify component placement and location on assemblies
proxy_refs: Reference designators (which component goes where on the assembly)

requirements.text: Apply effectivity dates to control when components are added to or removed from BOMs

requirements.text: Distinguish between Engineering BOM and Manufacturing BOM to support design and production workflows
proxy_refs: Engineering BOM vs Manufacturing BOM distinction

requirements.text: Calculate standard cost rollup from component costs to finished-good level
proxy_refs: Cost rollup from component costs to finished-good standard cost

requirements.text: Generate where-used reports to identify all BOMs and products that reference a given component
proxy_refs: Where-used report (find all BOMs that reference a given component)

capabilities.name: Routings, Operations & Work Centers
capabilities.description: Defines the sequence of operations required to produce an item, the work centers each operation runs on, and the standard times for setup, run, and teardown.

requirements.text: Routing defines an ordered sequence of operations required to manufacture or assemble a product
proxy_refs: Routing as an ordered sequence of operations

requirements.text: Each operation captures setup time, run time per piece, and teardown time
proxy_refs: Setup time, run time per piece, and teardown time

requirements.text: Routing supports parallel and alternate operation paths for flexible manufacturing
proxy_refs: Parallel and alternate operation paths

requirements.text: Multiple routing versions can be maintained with effectivity dates to manage engineering changes
proxy_refs: Routing versions with effectivity dates

requirements.text: Operations link to BOM components consumed or produced at that manufacturing step

requirements.text: Work instructions and technical documentation can be attached to each operation

imda_refs.text: A source of information on the sequence of processes involved to manufacture or assemble a product (e.g. technical drawing, Bill of Material (BOM), materials required and manufacturing process such as tools, equipment, instructions used to build the product

capabilities.name: Work Orders & Job Cards
capabilities.description: Create, schedule, execute, and close production work orders against BOMs and routings — the central operational artifact of a discrete manufacturing ERP.

capabilities.name: Material Requirements Planning (MRP)
capabilities.description: Net-requirements explosion from demand (sales orders + forecast) through BOMs and inventory policies, generating planned purchase and production orders to meet demand without stock-outs or excess.

requirements.text: Aggregate demand from sales orders, forecasts, and stock transfers into a consolidated demand schedule
proxy_refs: Demand aggregation from sales orders, forecasts, and stock transfers

proxy_refs: Multi-level BOM explosion net of on-hand and on-order quantities

requirements.text: Generate planned production orders for internally manufactured items based on net requirements
proxy_refs: Planned order generation for purchased items and manufactured items

requirements.text: Apply lot-sizing rules including fixed lot, lot-for-lot, economic order quantity, and period of supply methods
proxy_refs: Lot-size rules (fixed, lot-for-lot, economic, period of supply)

proxy_refs: Dependent demand visibility from finished good to raw material

capabilities.name: Production Scheduling & Capacity Planning
capabilities.description: Assign work orders to work centers across a time horizon, respecting finite capacity and operation sequence — distinct from MRP which generates orders without regard to capacity.

capabilities.name: Shop Floor Execution & MES

capabilities.name: Lot/Serial Traceability & Genealogy
capabilities.description: End-to-end forward and backward traceability from raw material lot through every production step to customer shipment — required for food, pharma, automotive, and aerospace. Supports recall.

requirements.text: Support forward traceability from raw material lot through all production steps to finished goods and customer shipment
proxy_refs: Forward trace (from raw lot → finished goods → customers)

requirements.text: Support backward traceability from finished goods to all input material lots and components used in production
proxy_refs: Backward trace (from finished good → all input lots)

requirements.text: Provide genealogy tree visualization showing material and component relationships across multiple assembly levels
proxy_refs: Genealogy tree visualisation across assembly levels

requirements.text: Maintain serial number as-built records for each finished unit, capturing all components and production parameters
proxy_refs: Serial number as-built record per finished unit

requirements.text: Track lot expiry dates and shelf-life constraints for materials and finished goods
proxy_refs: Lot expiry and shelf-life tracking

requirements.text: Generate recall lists by querying traceability data to identify all affected lots, serial numbers, and customer shipments
proxy_refs: Recall list generation from a trace query

requirements.text: Support regulatory traceability requirements including FDA 21 CFR Part 11, EU FMD, and FSMA compliance
proxy_refs: Regulatory traceability (FDA 21 CFR Part 11, EU FMD, FSMA)

requirements.text: Integrate with customer labeling and barcode standards including GS1 and UDI formats
proxy_refs: Integration with customer label and barcode standards (GS1, UDI)

capabilities.name: Repair, Rework & Returned-Goods Manufacturing
capabilities.description: Handle goods that come back from customers or fail internal quality, and need to go through a rework or repair process — distinct from normal production because the input is a finished good, not raw material.

requirements.text: Create and manage repair orders distinct from standard work orders, with separate identification and tracking
proxy_refs: Repair order distinct from work order

requirements.text: Support disassembly BOMs for tear-down and component inspection operations
proxy_refs: Disassembly BOM for tear-down operations

requirements.text: Enable selection and substitution of replacement components during repair execution
proxy_refs: Replacement-component selection during repair

requirements.text: Classify repairs as warranty or billable to support cost allocation and customer invoicing
proxy_refs: Warranty-vs-billable repair classification

requirements.text: Link repair orders to Return Merchandise Authorisation (RMA) records for traceability
proxy_refs: Return Merchandise Authorisation (RMA) linkage

requirements.text: Route and rework existing work-in-progress without requiring scrap or restart of production
proxy_refs: Rework routing on existing WIP without scrapping

requirements.text: Track repair costs separately from new-build manufacturing costs for financial reporting and analysis
proxy_refs: Cost-of-repair tracking separate from new-build cost

requirements.text: Capture and record failure analysis data on returned goods for quality and continuous improvement
proxy_refs: Failure-analysis capture on returned goods

capabilities.name: Maintenance Integration (CMMS / EAM Linkage)
capabilities.description: Bridge between the manufacturing module and the asset/maintenance module: planned downtime for PM, automatic work requests on breakdown, shared calendar for production scheduling.

requirements.text: Work center must be linked to a maintainable asset record to enable traceability between production and maintenance operations.
proxy_refs: Work center tied to a maintainable asset record

requirements.text: Planned preventive maintenance downtime must block or reserve capacity on the production calendar to prevent scheduling conflicts.
proxy_refs: Planned PM downtime blocks the production calendar

requirements.text: System must automatically generate maintenance requests when equipment breakdown or fault conditions are detected.
proxy_refs: Automatic maintenance request on machine fault / downtime reason

requirements.text: Production and maintenance work must be viewable in a unified calendar to enable coordinated scheduling and resource planning.
proxy_refs: Joint calendar view of production and maintenance work

requirements.text: Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) and Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) metrics must be visible to production planners for capacity and scheduling decisions.
proxy_refs: MTBF / MTTR visible to production planners

requirements.text: Operators must be able to trigger maintenance requests directly from the HMI when issues are detected.
proxy_refs: Maintenance requests trigger-able from operator HMI

requirements.text: Condition-based preventive maintenance must be automatically triggered based on meter readings or sensor data thresholds.
proxy_refs: Condition-based PM triggered by meter readings or sensor data

requirements.text: System must send notification alerts when production equipment experiences slowdown, breakdown, or disruption affecting production.

capabilities.name: Digital Thread & Manufacturing Digital Twin
capabilities.description: Continuous data linkage from design intent through as-planned to as-built, and a simulation model ("digital twin") of the production system used for scheduling, training, and continuous improvement.

requirements.text: Enable what-if scenario modeling for line rebalancing, bottleneck identification, and removal

requirements.text: Provide operator training simulation mode that mirrors production workflows without requiring live equipment access
proxy_refs: Operator training mode using the twin without touching real equipment

requirements.text: Implement closed-loop feedback mechanism to capture shop-floor data and propagate insights back to design and planning
proxy_refs: Closed-loop feedback from shop-floor data back to design
